evals.prompt: I have an sdd.md and spec.json for a loan approval case. Generate the implementation tasks from these spec files so I can build it.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, produces a tasks.md with ordered task entries covering case creation, stages, tasks, and conditions, then stops before Phase 2 because the request is plan-only
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
References uip maestro case registry pull for upfront caching
Produces a tasks.md file with numbered task sections (T01, T02, etc.)
Includes registry lookups to resolve taskTypeIds
Generates a registry-resolved.json for audit
Stops after tasks.md without creating a solution or caseplan because the request asks only for implementation tasks

evals.prompt: Break down my case spec into implementation tasks. The spec files are at ./specs/sdd.md and ./specs/spec.json
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, reads both spec files, resolves registry lookups, generates tasks.md, and stops before Phase 2 because the request is plan-only
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Reads both sdd.md and spec.json from the provided paths
Uses the component_type mapping to determine registry search types
Produces a tasks/ folder with tasks.md
Stops after tasks.md without creating a solution or caseplan because the request asks only for an implementation-task breakdown

evals.prompt: Convert this case specification into a step-by-step plan and build the case
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, produces an actionable tasks.md, then auto-proceeds to write caseplan.json directly via the matching plugins without a Phase 1 approval prompt
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Produces tasks.md with declarative task entries (no shell commands in tasks.md)
Cross-references use human-readable format like Stage.Task.field
Auto-proceeds from tasks.md into the build without asking for Phase 1 approval
Writes caseplan.json directly via plugin impl-json.md recipes

evals.prompt: I want to create a case management project
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, shows the new-case roadmap, and offers either an interview or a user-provided sdd.md path
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
Shows the journey-specific roadmap before detailed questions
Offers Interview to generate sdd.md, provide an sdd.md path, or abort

evals.prompt: Add a stage called 'Document Review' to my case.stage.json file
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger case-management planning workflow -- this is a direct stage edit on an existing case file
expectations: The case-management skill may be triggered for direct editing
Does NOT go through the sdd.md planning workflow

evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, emits caseplan.json with the top-level shape (id: case-<10>, version: 23.0.0, metadata, top-level bindings/variables, layout: {}), runs Phase 4 validate authoritatively
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered
caseplan.json top-level keys are: id, version, name, metadata, bindings, variables, nodes, edges, layout
caseplan.json.version === '23.0.0'
caseplan.json.id matches /^case-[A-Za-z0-9]{10}$/
No 'root' wrapper key in caseplan.json
metadata.caseExitRules is used once case-exit conditions are written
metadata.slaRules is used for root-target SLA
Top-level bindings and variables (not nested under any wrapper)
Stage and Trigger nodes have NO position, style, measured, width, height, zIndex (Rule 18)
Top-level layout: {} present and empty
Phase 4 validate runs authoritatively — 3-retry cap and hard stop on 3rd failure

evals.prompt: I want to create a new case but I do not have an sdd.md yet
evals.expected_output: Skill starts with a short journey-specific roadmap, opens the interview immediately, and confirms the inferred case in three plain-language packets before creating sdd.md
expectations: The case-management skill was triggered

Before detailed interview questions, shows the user the high-level journey: understand and ground, confirm in short summaries, generate and validate the SDD, build and review, then ask before debug or publish
Does not expose internal Phase 0 through Phase 6 labels in the user-facing roadmap
Uses three confirmation packets: case shape, work inside stages, and operational contract
The case-shape packet includes the trigger, primary stage flow, interrupting secondary stages, and case outcomes
The work packet includes task type, sequential/event-driven/manually-triggered activation, required/optional status, owner, and tenant resource candidate
The operational packet includes decision routing, data inputs/outputs, resolved resources/connections, and timing
A manually triggered task is presented as optional manually triggered work without exposing the internal adhoc rule name
The final approval summary is the second confirmation layer, so the user does not need to inspect the full sdd.md

evals.prompt: Create a vendor onboarding case. I attached two process documents. It uses VendorRiskAgent, Salesforce Prod, and a Slack notification connector.
evals.expected_output: Skill overlaps independent document and tenant discovery work, resolves tenant resources before the confirmation that depends on them, and keeps dependency-bound operations sequential
expectations: Opens the interview without waiting for tenant discovery to finish
After the user's first description, reads all supplied documents in parallel with the UiPath login-status check
Runs registry pull only after login succeeds
After a successful pull, searches all stable runnable-task candidates, connector operations/triggers, and enabled connection inventories in parallel
Does not search tenant caches before a successful Phase 0 pull and does not treat a failed pull as zero matches
Batches independent ambiguous resource and connection choices into one user turn when possible
After selections, runs tasks describe and case spec calls concurrently across all selected live resources
Fetches a unique high-confidence candidate's schema before presenting the work or operational confirmation that depends on it
Does not let tenant inventory add or rename business stages or tasks
Leaves Phase 1 registry refresh as the authoritative pre-planning gate

evals.prompt: Interview me, confirm your understanding, and generate the SDD only after I approve it
evals.expected_output: Skill checkpoints the draft in batches, validates the completed draft, and promotes it to sdd.md only after explicit final approval
expectations: Does not rewrite the complete sdd.draft.md after every answer; persists resumable state only at confirmed packet boundaries
Writes sdd.draft.md and tasks/registry-resolved.json independently and may persist them concurrently once their shared state is frozen
Runs the existing SDD finalization checks against sdd.draft.md before the final approval prompt
Shows a compact approval summary with main flow, interrupting secondary stages, task activation counts, resolved/unresolved resources, defaults, and review-item counts
Does not create or rename to sdd.md before the user selects approval
On correction, edits sdd.draft.md, revalidates affected checks, and re-shows the summary
On approval, atomically renames sdd.draft.md to sdd.md and then proceeds to Phase 1
